threat_model: A worker receipt ledger projection can accidentally become live receipt-store access or dispatch authority if read-model state is conflated with runtime execution. The main risks are reading a live database from validation, dispatching workers, emitting runtime receipts, calling connectors, serializing raw worker payloads or secrets, and treating a failure receipt as terminal closure. The mitigation is a schema-backed Foundation projection and validator that deny live reads, dispatch, receipt emission, connector authority, writes, terminal closure, and success claims while recomputing summary counts from bounded receipt refs.

required_checks.check: worker dispatch and runtime receipt emission remain denied
live receipt-store reads and connector calls remain denied
redaction check confirms raw payload and raw secret material remain denied
policy bypass check confirms terminal closure and success claims remain blocked per chain
receipt integrity check confirms status and contract summary counts cannot drift from projected receipt chains
required receipt refs and evidence refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms proof coverage matrix includes the read model surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode read model blocks live receipt-store reads, dispatch, connector calls, writes, terminal closure, and success claims.

mitigations: keep WorkerReceiptLedgerReadModel validation non-live and deterministic
deny live receipt-store reads and worker dispatch in Foundation Mode
deny runtime receipt emission and connector authority
reject raw payload and secret material
require recovery refs for recovery-required chains
reject terminal closure and success claims from read-model projections
